evals.name: eval-sar-logic
evals.prompt: Write a Verilog-A 10-bit SAR ADC logic block. It should take a comparator output (DCOMP) and a sample clock (CLKS), and generate 10 DAC control bits (DP_DAC[9:0]) plus a ready flag (RDY). Use rising edge of an internal bit-clock derived from CLKS. Include VDD and VSS as power ports.
evals.expected_output: A complete .va file with proper port declarations, genvar loops, @(initial_step), cross() events for clock edges, bit-cycling state machine, and transition() outputs using supply voltages.

evals.name: eval-vco
evals.prompt: I need a behavioral VCO for a PLL. Specs: 2 GHz center frequency, 200 MHz/V gain (Kvco), sinusoidal output, differential (OUTP/OUTN). Control voltage input is VCTR. Include phase noise via flicker_noise. Make sure the simulator doesn't skip cycles.
evals.expected_output: A .va file using idtmod() for phase accumulation, $bound_step() for time-step control, sin() output with differential pair, flicker_noise() call, and proper VDD/VSS power ports.

evals.name: eval-opamp
evals.prompt: Create an ideal opamp behavioral model in Verilog-A. Requirements: 80dB DC gain, 10MHz GBW, 1V/us slew rate, differential input (INP/INM), single-ended output (VOUT), rail-to-rail output clamping to VDD/VSS. Should model input resistance and output resistance.
evals.expected_output: A .va file with current-based modeling (I() <+), ddt() for dominant pole, slew-rate limiting via current clamp, output clamping to supply rails, computed internal parameters from specs in @(initial_step).

evals.name: eval-sar-12bit
evals.prompt: Write a 12-bit SAR ADC logic block with differential input, comparator interface, and CDAC control outputs. Include end-of-conversion flag.
evals.expected_output: A .va file with 12-bit SAR state machine, differential comparator interface, CDAC control bus, EOC flag, all 8 mandatory rules followed.

evals.name: eval-pipeline-stage
evals.prompt: Create a 1.5-bit pipeline ADC stage (MDAC) in Verilog-A. It should sample the input, compare against ±Vref/4 thresholds, compute the residue with gain-of-2, and output a 2-bit sub-ADC code.
evals.expected_output: A .va file modeling MDAC with two comparators, residue amplifier with gain-of-2, 2-bit thermometer output, sample-hold on clock phase.

evals.name: eval-segmented-dac
evals.prompt: I need a 14-bit DAC with 6-bit thermometer MSBs and 8-bit binary LSBs. Differential current-steering output. Include INL/DNL-friendly unary decoding for the MSBs.
evals.expected_output: A .va file with segmented architecture, 63 unary MSB elements, 256-level binary LSB, differential output around vcm.

evals.name: eval-cdac-cal
evals.prompt: Write a 10-bit capacitive DAC for a SAR ADC. Binary-weighted main array plus 2 redundant calibration capacitors. Differential topology (top-plate/bottom-plate). Model charge redistribution.
evals.expected_output: A .va file with capacitor weight array, charge redistribution math, calibration cap switching, differential top/bottom plate outputs.

evals.name: eval-multimod-divider
evals.prompt: Create a Verilog-A multi-modulus divider that can divide by N or N+1, controlled by a MOD input. Include prescaler output and a 4-bit modulus control word.
evals.expected_output: A .va file with counter-based divider, MOD-controlled modulus switching, prescaler output, digital bus input for ratio.

evals.name: eval-bbpd
evals.prompt: Write a bang-bang (binary) phase-frequency detector for a CDR. Three inputs: DATA, CLK, and RETIMED_DATA. Outputs: UP and DOWN pulses. Edge-triggered on DATA transitions.
evals.expected_output: A .va file with Alexander-type BBPD, cross() on DATA edges, early/late detection, UP/DOWN pulse generation.

evals.name: eval-charge-pump
evals.prompt: I need an ideal charge pump with configurable current mismatch. Parameters: Iup, Idown (separately settable), switching delay, and a leakage current parameter. Tri-state output when both UP and DOWN are low.
evals.expected_output: A .va file with separate Iup/Idown current sources, tri-state when idle, leakage path, transition() on current switching.

evals.name: eval-two-stage-opamp
evals.prompt: Model a two-stage opamp: first stage is a transconductor (gm1), second stage is a common-source (gm2) with Miller compensation capacitor Cc. Include both poles, the RHP zero, and output swing limiting.
evals.expected_output: A .va file with two internal nodes, gm1/gm2 stages, Miller cap (Cc) feedback, RHP zero via nulling resistor or feed-forward, output clamping.

evals.name: eval-sc-integrator
evals.prompt: Write a Verilog-A non-overlapping two-phase switched-cap integrator. Inputs: VIN, PHI1, PHI2. Output: VOUT. Parameters: Cin and Cfb capacitor values. Model the sample-then-integrate behavior.
evals.expected_output: A .va file with two-phase clock cross() events, charge transfer math (Cin*Vin/Cfb), held output between phases.

evals.name: eval-butterworth-lpf
evals.prompt: Create a 4th-order Butterworth low-pass filter using cascaded laplace_nd() biquad sections. Parameterize the cutoff frequency. Single input, single output.
evals.expected_output: A .va file with two cascaded laplace_nd() calls using Butterworth polynomial coefficients, parameterized fc.

evals.name: eval-therm2bin
evals.prompt: Write a Verilog-A thermometer-to-binary encoder. 15-bit thermometer input, 4-bit binary output. Handle bubble errors (non-monotonic thermometer codes) gracefully.
evals.expected_output: A .va file that counts '1' bits in thermometer code, converts to binary, handles bubble errors via majority or summation.

evals.name: eval-prbs7
evals.prompt: Create a 7-bit pseudo-random bit sequence (PRBS-7) generator in Verilog-A. Clock-driven, with a serial output and a parallel 7-bit state bus output. Use LFSR with XOR feedback taps at positions 7 and 6.
evals.expected_output: A .va file with 7-bit LFSR shift register, XOR feedback, serial and parallel outputs, clock-edge-driven.

evals.name: eval-clk-divider
evals.prompt: Write a programmable clock divider. 8-bit division ratio (1-255). 50% output duty cycle regardless of division ratio. Synchronous reset. Include a LOCK output that goes high after the first full output period.
evals.expected_output: A .va file with programmable counter, toggle logic for 50% duty, sync reset, LOCK flag after first complete cycle.

evals.name: eval-multitone
evals.prompt: Create a signal source that outputs the sum of N sinusoids. Parameters: an array of frequencies and amplitudes (up to 8 tones). Include $bound_step for the highest frequency component.
evals.expected_output: A .va file with frequency/amplitude parameter arrays, summation loop, $bound_step tied to max frequency, $abstime-based generation.

evals.name: eval-nrz-prbs
evals.prompt: Write a Verilog-A NRZ data generator for SerDes testing. PRBS-15 pattern, configurable data rate (default 10 Gbps), output amplitude, and pre-emphasis (1-tap FIR). Differential output.
evals.expected_output: A .va file with 15-bit LFSR, data-rate clock via $abstime or timer, FIR pre-emphasis (main + post cursor), differential output.

evals.name: eval-varactor
evals.prompt: Model a MOS varactor in Verilog-A. C(V) characteristic: C = Cmin + (Cmax-Cmin)/2 * (1 + tanh((V-V0)/Vslope)). Two terminals. Include quality factor via series resistance.
evals.expected_output: A .va file with voltage-dependent capacitance via tanh(), I() <+ ddt(C(V)*V), series resistance for Q factor.

evals.name: eval-ldo
evals.prompt: Create a behavioral low-dropout regulator. Input: VIN (unregulated). Output: VOUT (regulated). Parameters: target voltage, dropout voltage, PSRR (as a 1st-order HPF), output impedance, current limit. Clamp output to VIN-Vdropout when input is too low.
evals.expected_output: A .va file with regulation loop, dropout clamping, PSRR via laplace or ddt filter, output impedance, current limiting.

evals.name: eval-bg-cal
evals.prompt: Write a background calibration module that measures comparator offset via a chopping technique. Inputs: COMP_OUT, CLK. Output: 6-bit trim code (TRIM[5:0]). It should average N comparisons, compute the offset direction, and adjust the trim code by ±1 LSB each calibration cycle. Include a SETTLED output flag when trim code stops changing for 8 consecutive cycles.
evals.expected_output: A .va file with accumulator-based averaging, chopping logic, ±1 step adjustment, settled counter, digital bus output with transition().

evals.name: eval-mosfet-sq
evals.prompt: Write a simple square-law MOSFET model: 4 terminals (D,G,S,B). Model linear and saturation regions with body effect. Parameters: Kp, Vth0, lambda, gamma, phi. Include subthreshold region with exponential rolloff.
evals.expected_output: A .va file with 4-terminal MOSFET, region-based I-V (cutoff/linear/saturation), body effect via gamma, subthreshold exponential.

evals.name: eval-inl-dnl-probe
evals.prompt: Create a testbench probe that monitors a DAC output and computes code-by-code INL and DNL. Input: analog DAC output + clock strobe. Output: writes results to a file using $fopen/$fdisplay at end of simulation.
evals.expected_output: A .va file with code-stepping on strobe, voltage capture array, DNL/INL computation at final_step, file I/O output.

evals.name: eval-mixed-charge-pump-pfd
evals.prompt: Write a single Verilog-A module that combines a phase-frequency detector (PFD) and charge pump. The PFD uses @(cross()) for edge detection on REF and FB clocks, generating UP/DOWN signals. The charge pump uses I(OUT) <+ to inject/sink current based on UP/DOWN state. Include dead-zone prevention and current mismatch parameters.
evals.expected_output: The module should be REJECTED as mixed-domain. It combines @(cross()) (voltage-domain) with I() <+ (current-domain). The correct approach is to split into two modules: a voltage-domain PFD and a current-domain charge pump core.

evals.name: eval-mixed-ldo-digital-control
evals.prompt: Create a single Verilog-A LDO module with an integrated digital controller. The digital controller uses @(cross()) to detect when VOUT drops below target, a counter-based FSM with transition() outputs for trim code adjustment, and the analog regulator section uses I(VOUT) <+ for the pass device current and laplace_nd() for the control loop.
evals.expected_output: The module should be REJECTED as mixed-domain. It combines @(cross())/transition() (voltage-domain) with I() <+/laplace_nd() (current-domain). The correct approach is to split into a voltage-domain digital controller and a current-domain analog regulator.

evals.name: eval-mixed-sar-with-cdac-current
evals.prompt: Write a single Verilog-A module for a complete SAR ADC that includes the SAR logic (using @(cross()) for clock edges, genvar for bit loops, transition() for DAC control outputs) and an embedded CDAC that models charge redistribution using I(top_plate) <+ ddt(C * V(top_plate, bot_plate)).
evals.expected_output: The module should be REJECTED as mixed-domain. It combines @(cross())/transition()/genvar (voltage-domain) with I() <+/ddt() (current-domain). The correct approach is to split into a voltage-domain SAR logic module and a current-domain CDAC module.

evals.name: eval-d2b-4bit
evals.prompt: Write a 4-bit static analog-to-binary converter module named _va_d2b_4b. Input: analog voltage VIN (range VSS to VDD). Output: 4-bit binary code DOUT[3:0]. No clock — purely combinational, continuous tracking. Use VDD/VSS power ports.
evals.expected_output: A .va file with ANSI-inline or old-style port declarations compatible with Spectre, voltage normalization, integer quantization to 16 levels, bit extraction via shift/mask in genvar loop, transition() output with supply voltages. Reference: assets/examples-archive/adc-sar/_va_d2b_4b.va
